result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  azputils [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  runagent
  code, dev-code-server, vscode
  powershell, pwsh <command>     []
  storage
  info                           Display info.
  runtaskcmd                     Run command until it completes or build finishes. Also completes agent invocation task.
  run                            Run command.
  synchronize                    Synchronize following steps in parallel jobs
  gz                             Compresses the given file using gzip to the given path
  extract-log                    Extract variables from a log file
  update-record                  Updates the given timeline record
  download-log                   Downloads the log to a file
  copy-log                       Copies a reference to the log under another job
  reserve                        Reserve a slot for the specified task

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  azputils [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  runagent
  code, dev-code-server, vscode
  powershell, pwsh <command>     []
  storage
  info                           Display info.
  runtaskcmd                     Run command until it completes or build finishes. Also completes agent invocation task.
  run                            Run command.
  synchronize                    Synchronize following steps in parallel jobs
  gz                             Compresses the given file using gzip to the given path
  extract-log                    Extract variables from a log file
  update-record                  Updates the given timeline record
  download-log                   Downloads the log to a file
  copy-log                       Copies a reference to the log under another job
  reserve                        Reserve a slot for the specified task

result.stdout:
Description:
  Copies a reference to the log under another job

Usage:
  azputils copy-log [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)             annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)                 The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --phaseId <phaseId>                        The phase id (e.g. $(System.PhaseId))
  --name <name> (REQUIRED)                   The name of the task
  --prepend <prepend>                        The header line(s) to add to the log
  --format-headers                           Causes header lines to be formatted along with log lines [default: False]
  --prefix <prefix>                          The prefix to add to each line.
  --escaping <escaping>                      The escaping rules to apply to the line text
  --format <format>                          The format string to replace token in
  --replacement-token <replacement-token>    The replacement token in the format string [default: $(Line)]
  --parent-job-name <parent-job-name>        The name or id of the parent job
  --start-line <start-line>                  The start line of the logs
  --end-line <end-line>                      The end line of the logs
  --start-line-pattern <start-line-pattern>  The start line regex pattern of the logs
  --end-line-pattern <end-line-pattern>      The end line pattern of the logs
  --source-id <source-id>                    The id of the source task logs to copy
  --target-id <target-id>                    The id of the target task logs to create or replace
  --copy-state                               Whether to copy the full state of the source record [default: False]
  --order <order>                            The order of the created task
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Copies a reference to the log under another job

Usage:
  azputils copy-log [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)             annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)                 The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --phaseId <phaseId>                        The phase id (e.g. $(System.PhaseId))
  --name <name> (REQUIRED)                   The name of the task
  --prepend <prepend>                        The header line(s) to add to the log
  --format-headers                           Causes header lines to be formatted along with log lines [default: False]
  --prefix <prefix>                          The prefix to add to each line.
  --escaping <escaping>                      The escaping rules to apply to the line text
  --format <format>                          The format string to replace token in
  --replacement-token <replacement-token>    The replacement token in the format string [default: $(Line)]
  --parent-job-name <parent-job-name>        The name or id of the parent job
  --start-line <start-line>                  The start line of the logs
  --end-line <end-line>                      The end line of the logs
  --start-line-pattern <start-line-pattern>  The start line regex pattern of the logs
  --end-line-pattern <end-line-pattern>      The end line pattern of the logs
  --source-id <source-id>                    The id of the source task logs to copy
  --target-id <target-id>                    The id of the target task logs to create or replace
  --copy-state                               Whether to copy the full state of the source record [default: False]
  --order <order>                            The order of the created task
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Downloads the log to a file

Usage:
  azputils download-log [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)             annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --target <target>                          The target file or null to output to standard out
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)                 The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --prepend <prepend>                        The header line(s) to add to the log
  --format-headers                           Causes header lines to be formatted along with log lines [default: False]
  --prefix <prefix>                          The prefix to add to each line.
  --escaping <escaping>                      The escaping rules to apply to the line text
  --format <format>                          The format string to replace token in
  --replacement-token <replacement-token>    The replacement token in the format string [default: $(Line)]
  --start-line <start-line>                  The start line of the logs
  --end-line <end-line>                      The end line of the logs
  --start-line-pattern <start-line-pattern>  The start line regex pattern of the logs
  --end-line-pattern <end-line-pattern>      The end line pattern of the logs
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Extract variables from a log file

Usage:
  azputils extract-log [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)                       annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)                           The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --patterns <patterns> (REQUIRED)                     The patterns for extraction
  --missing-behavior <Empty|EnvironmentFallback|Skip>  The behavior for missing values [default: Skip]
  --source-id <source-id>                              The id of the source task logs to process
  --start-line <start-line>                            The start line of the logs
  --end-line <end-line>                                The end line of the logs
  --secret
  --output
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

Usage:

Options:

  --target <target> (REQUIRED)                                      The target file path
  --overwrite                                                       [default: False]
  --compression-level <Fastest|NoCompression|Optimal|SmallestSize>  [default: Fastest]
  -?, -h, --help                                                    Show help and usage information

  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)  annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )

result.stdout:
Description:
  Reserve a slot for the specified task

Usage:
  azputils reserve [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)    annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)        The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --jobCount <jobCount> (REQUIRED)  The number of job slots available for reservation
  --agentName <agentName>           The name of the agent
  --pollSeconds <pollSeconds>       [default: 5]
  --checkOnly
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  azputils runagent [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)                  annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )

  --agent-directory <agent-directory> (REQUIRED)  The agent download directory
  --pool <pool> (REQUIRED)                        The agent pool name
  --organization-url <organization-url>           The agent pool organization url
  --name <name>                                   The agent name
  --package-url <package-url>                     The agent package download url
  --package-path <package-path>                   The agent package source path
  --clean <Agent|All|None|Work>                   Whether to clean the agent directory
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Run command until it completes or build finishes. Also completes agent invocation task.

Usage:
  azputils runtaskcmd [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)     annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)         The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --pollSeconds <pollSeconds>        [default: 5]
  --retries <retries>                [default: 3]
  --timeoutSeconds <timeoutSeconds>
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

  --expiry <expiry> (REQUIRED)                  The sas expiry time.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Synchronize following steps in parallel jobs

Usage:
  azputils synchronize [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)      annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)          The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --jobCount <jobCount> (REQUIRED)    The number of job slots available for synchronization (e.g. $(System.TotalJobsInPhase) )
  --qualifier <qualifier> (REQUIRED)  The key used to correlated between multiple synchronizations in the same job phase []
  phaseId, --recordId <recordId>      The phase id (e.g. $(System.PhaseId))
  --timeout <timeout>                 The timeout
  --recordId-key <recordId-key>       The build property key containing the record id to use for synchronization
  --complete                          Whether to complete the task referred to by the record id [default: False]
  --waitOnly                          Whether to only wait for the synchronization and not participate [default: False]
  --scope <Default|Task>              The synchronization scope
  --name <name>                       The name of the job (e.g. $(System.JobDisplayName) )
  --pollSeconds <pollSeconds>         [default: 5]
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Synchronize following steps in parallel jobs

Usage:
  azputils synchronize [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)      annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)          The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --jobCount <jobCount> (REQUIRED)    The number of job slots available for synchronization (e.g. $(System.TotalJobsInPhase) )
  --qualifier <qualifier> (REQUIRED)  The key used to correlated between multiple synchronizations in the same job phase []
  phaseId, --recordId <recordId>      The phase id (e.g. $(System.PhaseId))
  --timeout <timeout>                 The timeout
  --recordId-key <recordId-key>       The build property key containing the record id to use for synchronization
  --complete                          Whether to complete the task referred to by the record id [default: False]
  --waitOnly                          Whether to only wait for the synchronization and not participate [default: False]
  --scope <Default|Task>              The synchronization scope
  --name <name>                       The name of the job (e.g. $(System.JobDisplayName) )
  --pollSeconds <pollSeconds>         [default: 5]
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Updates the given timeline record

Usage:
  azputils update-record [options]

Options:
  --taskUrl <taskUrl> (REQUIRED)                                              annotated build task uri (e.g. $(System.CollectionUri)$(System.TeamProject)?buildId=$(Build.BuildId)&jobId=$(System.JobId)&planId=$(System.PlanId)&timelineId=$(System.TimelineId)&taskId=$(System.TaskInstanceId) )
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)                                                  The access token (e.g. $(System.AccessToken) )
  --id <id>                                                                   The id of the record
  --name <name>                                                               The name of the task
  --parent-id <parent-id>                                                     The parent id of the created record
  --type <Job|Phase|Stage|Task>                                               The record type
  --result <Abandoned|Canceled|Failed|Skipped|Succeeded|SucceededWithIssues>  The result
  --progress <progress>                                                       The percent complete
  --secrets <secrets>                                                         The secret variable names to upsert
  --variables <variables>                                                     The non-secret variable names to upsert
  --variable-input-prefix <variable-input-prefix>                             The prefix of variables to upsert
  --variable-output-prefix <variable-output-prefix>                           The prefix added to upserted variables
  --debug
  -?, -h, --help                                                              Show help and usage information
